Viewpoint along the California Coastal Trail at The Presidio of San Francisco. Beautiful views of the Golden Gate Bridge and the Marin Headlands beyond as well as the Pacific Ocean coastline. The trail is easy to hike and begins at the Golden Gate Welcome Center and ends at Baker's Beach.

Pacific Overlook is one of those places that feels tucked away but delivers major scenery. You get sweeping views of the Pacific Ocean, the Golden Gate Bridge, and even a peek at the Bay Bridge on clear days. It’s part of the Coastal Trail, so it’s easy to reach on foot, and there’s usually space to pause without the crowds.

Whether you’re snapping a family photo or just catching your breath mid-hike, it’s a peaceful stop with postcard-worthy angles. No ticket needed, no fuss—just nature doing its thing.
